Name: /sys:system-state/nokia-ietf-system-aug:system-ip/nokia-ietf-system-aug:ipv4
Description: System IPv4 Address. Default value is the 0.0.0.0

Name: /sys:system-state/nokia-ietf-system-aug:system-ip/nokia-ietf-system-aug:ipv6
Description: System IPv6 Address. Default value is the ::

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-qos-pol-s:qos-policies/bbf-qos-pol-s:ingress/bbf-qos-pol-s:policy/bbf-qos-pol-s:classifier/nokia-flow-stats:index
Description: The index of the classifier.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-qos-pol-s:qos-policies/bbf-qos-pol-s:egress/bbf-qos-pol-s:policy/bbf-qos-pol-s:classifier/nokia-flow-stats:index
Description: The index of the classifier.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:name
Description: The name associated with this software. For a given  physical entity, there may be more than one type of  software being managed, e.g. application software,  firmware or FPGA firmware. The name should represent  this association, e.g. 'firmware'.

Name: /bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-state/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-databases/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-database/bbf-l2-fwd:mac-addresses/bbf-l2-fwd:mac-address/nokia-sdan-l2-fwd-meta-ref:hiearchy-meta-info-reference
Description: Hierarchy meta info data.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:revisions/bbf-sim:revision/bbf-sim:name
Description: The name of the software revision. This is the name used  when the software was downloaded, e.g. filename.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:revisions/bbf-sim:revision/bbf-sim:is-active
Description: Reports whether the associated software revision is  active ('true') or inactive ('false').

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:revisions/bbf-sim:revision/bbf-sim:version
Description: Reports the version of the software revision.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:revisions/bbf-sim:revision/bbf-sim:is-valid
Description: Reports whether the stored software revision is  valid ('true') or invalid ('false').

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:revisions/bbf-sim:revision/bbf-sim:is-committed
Description: Reports whether the associated software revision is  committed ('true') or uncommitted ('false').

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:software/bbf-sim:revisions/bbf-sim:revision/bbf-sim:download-timestamp
Description: The time and date when the software was downloaded.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/nokia-hw-ext:local-network-address
Description: Address assigned to chassis or board when connected to a local network,  address assigned to chassis indicates system mac.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:cache/nokia-ipfix:stored-data-records
Description: Indicates the number of data-records that are maintained in memory for this cache, due to the ongoing connectivity loss  (applicable only to caches marked as conveying critical-content)

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:cache/nokia-ipfix:dropped-data-records
Description: Indicates the number of data-records that have been dropped for this cache, due to the ongoing connectivity loss  (applicable only to caches marked as conveying critical-content)

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:cache/nokia-ipfix:total-dropped-data-records
Description: Indicates the total number of data-records that have been dropped for this cache, during connectivity losses  (applicable only to caches marked as conveying critical-content)

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:diagnostics/bbf-hw-xcvr:temperature
Description: Internally measured transceiver temperature Temperature  accuracy is vendor specific but must be better than +/- 3  degrees Celsius over specified operating temperature and  voltage.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:diagnostics/bbf-hw-xcvr:supply-voltage
Description: Internally measured transceiver supply voltage. Practical  considerations to be defined by transceiver manufacturer  will tend to limit the actual bounds of the supply voltage  measurement. Accuracy is vendor specific but must be better  than +/- 3% of the manufacturer's nominal value over  specified operating temperature and voltage. Note that in  some transceivers, transmitter supply voltage and receiver  supply voltage are isolated. In that case, only one supply  is monitored. Refer to the device specification for more  detail.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:temperature-high-alarm
Description: The temperature above which will trigger a high temperature  alarm.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:temperature-low-alarm
Description: The temperature below which will trigger a low temperature  alarm.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:temperature-high-warning
Description: The temperature above which will trigger a high temperature  warning.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:temperature-low-warning
Description: The temperature below which will trigger a low temperature  warning.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:supply-voltage-high-alarm
Description: The voltage above which will trigger a high supply voltage  alarm.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:supply-voltage-low-alarm
Description: The voltage below which will trigger a low supply voltage  alarm.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:supply-voltage-high-warning
Description: The voltage above which will trigger a high supply voltage  warning.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:supply-voltage-low-warning
Description: The voltage below which will trigger a low supply voltage  warning.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:tx-bias-high-alarm
Description: The bias current above which will trigger a high transmit  bias alarm.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:tx-bias-low-alarm
Description: The bias current below which will trigger a low transmit  bias alarm.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:tx-bias-high-warning
Description: The bias current above which will trigger a high transmit  bias warning.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:tx-bias-low-warning
Description: The bias current below which will trigger a low transmit  bias warning.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:tx-power-high-alarm
Description: The power above which will trigger a high transmit  power alarm.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:tx-power-low-alarm
Description: The power below which will trigger a low transmit  power alarm.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:tx-power-high-warning
Description: The power above which will trigger a high transmit  power warning.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:tx-power-low-warning
Description: The power below which will trigger a low transmit  power warning.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:rx-power-high-alarm
Description: The power above which will trigger a high receive  power alarm.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:rx-power-low-alarm
Description: The power below which will trigger a low receive  power alarm.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:rx-power-high-warning
Description: The power above which will trigger a high receive  power warning.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:thresholds/bbf-hw-xcvr:rx-power-low-warning
Description: The power below which will trigger a low receive  power warning.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver-link/bbf-hw-xcvr:diagnostics/bbf-hw-xcvr:tx-bias
Description: Measured TX bias current in mA. Accuracy is vendor specific  but must be better than +/- 10% of the manufacturer's  nominal value over specified operating temperature and  voltage.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:number-of-intervals
Description: The total number of 15-minute PM intervals for which data  was collected.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:non-valid-intervals
Description: The number of 15-minute PM intervals for which the data  is considered to be invalid or incomplete.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:interval-number
Description: The number of the interval relative to the current  interval.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:measured-time
Description: Reports the amount of time, measured in seconds,  that statistics for this interval have been counted.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:invalid-data-flag
Description: Reports the validity of the corresponding PM data.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:time-stamp
Description: Reports the start date/time for this interval.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:in-octets
Description: The total number of octets received on the interface,  including framing characters.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:in-unicast-pkts
Description: The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a  higher (sub-)layer, that were not addressed to a  multicast or broadcast address at this sub-layer.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:in-broadcast-pkts
Description: The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a  higher (sub-)layer, that were addressed to a broadcast  address at this sub-layer.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:in-multicast-pkts
Description: The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a  higher (sub-)layer, that were addressed to a multicast  address at this sub-layer.  For a MAC-layer protocol,  this includes both Group and Functional addresses.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:in-discards
Description: The number of inbound packets that were chosen to be  discarded even though no errors had been detected to  prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer  protocol.  One possible reason for discarding such a  packet could be to free up buffer space.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:out-octets
Description: The total number of octets transmitted out of the  interface, including framing characters.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:out-unicast-pkts
Description: The total number of packets that higher-level protocols  requested be transmitted, and that were not addressed  to a multicast or broadcast address at this sub-layer,  including those that were discarded or not sent.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:out-broadcast-pkts
Description: The total number of packets that higher-level protocols  requested be transmitted, and that were addressed to a  broadcast address at this sub-layer, including those  that were discarded or not sent.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:out-multicast-pkts
Description: The total number of packets that higher-level protocols  requested be transmitted, and that were addressed to a  multicast address at this sub-layer, including those  that were discarded or not sent.  For a MAC-layer  protocol, this includes both Group and Functional  addresses.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/bbf-if-pm:out-discards
Description: The number of outbound packets that were chosen to be  discarded even though no errors had been detected to  prevent their being transmitted.  One possible reason  for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer  space.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-if-ctrs:in-pkts
Description: The total number of packets received on the interface.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-if-ctrs:out-pkts
Description: The total number of packets transmitted out of the  interface.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-l2-d4r:dhcpv4/bbf-l2-d4r:in-bad-packets-from-client
Description: Bad DHCPv4 packets received on the interface. The packets  are received from a DHCPv4 Client connected to it (directly  or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  L2 DHCPv4 Relay functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-l2-d4r:dhcpv4/bbf-l2-d4r:in-bad-packets-from-server
Description: Bad DHCPv4 packets received on the interface. The packets  are received from a DHCPv4 Server connected to it (directly  or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  L2 DHCPv4 Relay functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-l2-d4r:dhcpv4/bbf-l2-d4r:in-packets-from-client
Description: Valid DHCPv4 packets received on the interface. The packets  are received from a DHCPv4 Client connected to it (directly  or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  L2 DHCPv4 Relay functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-l2-d4r:dhcpv4/bbf-l2-d4r:in-packets-from-server
Description: Valid DHCPv4 packets received on the interface. The packets  are received from a DHCPv4 Server connected to it (directly  or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  L2 DHCPv4 Relay functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-l2-d4r:dhcpv4/bbf-l2-d4r:out-packets-to-server
Description: Valid DHCPv4 packets sent to the interface. The packets are  sent to a DHCPv4 Server connected to it (directly or  indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  L2 DHCPv4 Relay functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-l2-d4r:dhcpv4/bbf-l2-d4r:out-packets-to-client
Description: Valid DHCPv4 packets sent to the interface. The packets are  sent to a DHCPv4 Client connected to it (directly or  indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  L2 DHCPv4 Relay functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-l2-d4r:dhcpv4/bbf-l2-d4r:option-82-inserted-packets-to-server
Description: DHCPv4 packets from a Client to a Server that pass this  interface and into which the L2 DHCPv4 Relay inserted  option 82.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  L2 DHCPv4 Relay functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-l2-d4r:dhcpv4/bbf-l2-d4r:option-82-removed-packets-to-client
Description: DHCPv4 packets from a Server to a Client that pass this  interface and from which the L2 DHCPv4 Relay removed  option 82.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  L2 DHCPv4 Relay functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-l2-d4r:dhcpv4/bbf-l2-d4r:option-82-not-inserted-to-server
Description: DHCPv4 packets from a Client to a Server that pass this  interface and for which the L2 DHCPv4 Relay did not insert  option 82 because after insertion the length would exceed  the maximum allowed packet size.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  L2 DHCPv4 Relay functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-state/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarders/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarder/bbf-l2-fwd:name
Description: The name of the forwarder.

Name: /bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-state/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarders/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarder/bbf-l2-fwd:ports/bbf-l2-fwd:port/bbf-l2-fwd:name
Description: The name of the forwarder port.

Name: /bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-state/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarders/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarder/bbf-l2-fwd:ports/bbf-l2-fwd:port/bbf-l2-fwd:sub-interface
Description: The VLAN sub-interface associated with this port.

Name: /bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-state/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarders/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarder/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-databases/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-database
Description: A reference to a forwarding database associated with a  forwarder.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:name
Description: This object is the name associated with a particular  multicast VPN (service context).

Name: /bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-state/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-databases/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-database/bbf-l2-fwd:name
Description: The name of the forwarding database.

Name: /bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-state/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-databases/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-database/bbf-l2-fwd:mac-addresses/bbf-l2-fwd:mac-address/bbf-l2-fwd:mac-address
Description: A specific MAC address that is stored in the  forwarding database.

Name: /bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-state/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-databases/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-database/bbf-l2-fwd:mac-addresses/bbf-l2-fwd:mac-address/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarder
Description: This leaf references a forwarder.

Name: /bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-state/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-databases/bbf-l2-fwd:forwarding-database/bbf-l2-fwd:mac-addresses/bbf-l2-fwd:mac-address/bbf-l2-fwd:port
Description: This leaf references a port within the forwarder identified  by the leaf 'forwarder'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-ldra:dhcpv6-ldra/bbf-ldra:in-bad-packets-from-client
Description: Bad DHCPv6 packets received on the interface. The packets  are received from a DHCPv6 Client connected to it (directly  or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  DHCPv6 LDRA functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-ldra:dhcpv6-ldra/bbf-ldra:in-bad-packets-from-server
Description: Bad DHCPv6 packets received on the interface. The packets  are received from a DHCPv6 Server connected to it (directly  or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  DHCPv6 LDRA functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-ldra:dhcpv6-ldra/bbf-ldra:option-17-inserted-packets-to-server
Description: DHCPv6 packets from a Client to a Server that passed this  interface and for which the DHCPv6 LDRA did insert  option 17.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  DHCPv6 LDRA functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-ldra:dhcpv6-ldra/bbf-ldra:option-17-removed-packets-to-client
Description: DHCPv6 packets from a Server to a Client that passed this  interface and for which the DHCPv6 LDRA did remove  option 17.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  DHCPv6 LDRA functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-ldra:dhcpv6-ldra/bbf-ldra:option-18-inserted-packets-to-server
Description: DHCPv6 packets from a Client to a Server that passed this  interface and for which the DHCPv6 LDRA did insert  option 18.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  DHCPv6 LDRA functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-ldra:dhcpv6-ldra/bbf-ldra:option-18-removed-packets-to-client
Description: DHCPv6 packets from a Server to a Client that passed this  interface and for which the DHCPv6 LDRA did remove  option 18.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  DHCPv6 LDRA functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-ldra:dhcpv6-ldra/bbf-ldra:option-37-inserted-packets-to-server
Description: DHCPv6 packets from a Client to a Server that passed this  interface and for which the DHCPv6 LDRA did insert  option 37.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  DHCPv6 LDRA functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-ldra:dhcpv6-ldra/bbf-ldra:option-37-removed-packets-to-client
Description: DHCPv6 packets from a Server to a Client that passed this  interface and for which the DHCPv6 LDRA did remove  option 37.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  DHCPv6 LDRA functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-ldra:dhcpv6-ldra/bbf-ldra:outgoing-mtu-exceeded-packets-from-client
Description: DHCPv6 packets from a Client that are dropped because the  Relay-Message size after adding the options would exceed  the MTU of the outgoing interface.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  DHCPv6 LDRA functionality is disabled/enabled.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-interface-to-host/bbf-mgmd:name
Description: The name of the multicast interface to a host.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-interface-to-host/bbf-mgmd:interface-to-host-rx-state-data/bbf-mgmd:in-successful-join-requests
Description: Counts the total number of successful join requests received  from hosts connected to the interface. This includes new  joins (which make a new connection) and re-joins (which  already have a connection).    For MGMD version 2 it counts the number of group membership  report messages that resulted in a successful join.    For MGMD version 3 it counts the number of successful joins  that are the result of receiving group membership  report messages on this interface. Note that a single group  membership report message can contain multiple records for  different group addresses and therefore can result in  multiple increments of this counter.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-interface-to-host/bbf-mgmd:interface-to-host-rx-state-data/bbf-mgmd:in-unsuccessful-join-requests
Description: Counts the total number of unsuccessful new join requests  from hosts connected to the interface. The failures could be  due to the following reasons: permission bitmap failure,  bitrate failure, lack of resource or invalid group address.    For MGMD version 2 it counts the number of group membership  report messages that failed to result in a successful join.    For MGMD version 3 it counts the number of unsuccessful joins  that are the result of receiving group membership  report messages on this interface. Note that a single group  membership report message can contain multiple records for  different group addresses and therefore can result in  multiple increments of this counter.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-interface-to-host/bbf-mgmd:interface-to-host-rx-state-data/bbf-mgmd:in-leaves
Description: For MGMD version 2 it counts the number of leave group  messages received on this interface, i.e. messages that are  requesting to leave a multicast group address.    For MGMD version 3 it counts the number of leaves  that are the result of receiving group membership  report messages on this interface. Note that a single group  membership report message can contain multiple records for  different group addresses and therefore can result in  multiple increments of this counter.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-interface-to-host/bbf-mgmd:interface-to-host-rx-state-data/bbf-mgmd:in-invalid-messages
Description: Counts the number of invalid MGMD messages received on this  interface.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-interface-to-host/bbf-mgmd:interface-to-hosts-tx-state-data/bbf-mgmd:out-specific-queries
Description: Counts the number of group-specific query messages sent on  this interface.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-interface-to-host/bbf-mgmd:interface-to-hosts-tx-state-data/bbf-mgmd:out-general-queries
Description: Counts the number of general query messages sent on this  interface.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-network-interface/bbf-mgmd:name
Description: The name of the multicast network interface.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-network-interface/bbf-mgmd:single-uplink-interface-to-router-state-data/bbf-mgmd:vlan-sub-interface
Description: This object specifies the VLAN sub-interface via  which the multicast channel is received in the  multicast switch.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-network-interface/bbf-mgmd:single-uplink-interface-to-router-state-data/bbf-mgmd:out-joins
Description: For MGMD version 2 it counts the number of group membership  report messages sent on this interface, i.e. messages that  are requesting to join a multicast group address.    For MGMD version 3 it counts the number of group membership  report messages sent on this interface that contain group  records with a record type that results in joining a  multicast group. I.e. the version 3 way of joining a  multicast group address.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-network-interface/bbf-mgmd:single-uplink-interface-to-router-state-data/bbf-mgmd:out-leaves
Description: For MGMD version 2 it counts the number of leave group  messages sent on this interface, i.e. messages that are  requesting to leave a multicast group address.    For MGMD version 3 it counts the number of group membership  report messages sent on this interface that contain group  records with record type that results in leaving a multicast  group. I.e. the version 3 way of leaving a multicast group  address.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-network-interface/bbf-mgmd:single-uplink-interface-to-router-state-data/bbf-mgmd:in-general-queries
Description: Counts the number of general query messages received on this  interface.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-network-interface/bbf-mgmd:single-uplink-interface-to-router-state-data/bbf-mgmd:in-specific-queries
Description: Counts the number of group-specific query messages received  on this interface.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-network-interface/bbf-mgmd:single-uplink-interface-to-router-state-data/bbf-mgmd:in-invalid-message
Description: Counts the number of invalid messages received on the  interface.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-network-interface/bbf-mgmd:single-uplink-interface-to-router-state-data/bbf-mgmd:in-interface-wrong-version-queries
Description: The number of general queries received whose IGMP or MLD  version does not match the version configured in the  VPN its protocol version.    Both IGMP and MLD require that all routers on a LAN  be configured to run the same version.  Thus, if any general  queries are received with the wrong version, this indicates  a configuration error.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-network-interface/bbf-mgmd:single-uplink-interface-to-router-state-data/bbf-mgmd:host-interface-version2-querier-time
Description: The time remaining until the host assumes that there are no  MGMDv2 routers present on the interface. While this is  non-zero, the host will reply to all queries with version 2  membership reports. This variable applies to MGMDv3 hosts  that are forced to run in v2 for compatibility with v2 hosts  or routers present on the interface.

Name: /bbf-mgmd:multicast-state/bbf-mgmd:mgmd/bbf-mgmd:multicast-vpn/bbf-mgmd:multicast-proxy-or-snooper/bbf-mgmd:number-active-groups
Description: Counts the total number of active multicast channels in  the VPN.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-pppoe-ia:pppoe/bbf-pppoe-ia:in-error-packets-from-client
Description: Invalid PPPoE discovery stage packets received on the  interface. The packets are received from a PPPoE Client  connected to it (directly or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  PPPoE Intermediate Agent functionality is disabled/  enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-pppoe-ia:pppoe/bbf-pppoe-ia:in-error-packets-from-server
Description: Invalid PPPoE discovery stage packets received on the  interface. The packets are received from a PPPoE Server  connected to it (directly or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  PPPoE Intermediate Agent functionality is disabled/  enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-pppoe-ia:pppoe/bbf-pppoe-ia:in-packets-from-client
Description: Valid PPPoE discovery stage packets received on the  interface. The packets are received from a PPPoE Client  connected to it (directly or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  PPPoE Intermediate Agent functionality is disabled/  enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-pppoe-ia:pppoe/bbf-pppoe-ia:in-packets-from-server
Description: Valid PPPoE discovery stage packets received on the  interface. The packets are received from a PPPoE Server  connected to it (directly or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  PPPoE Intermediate Agent functionality is disabled/  enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-pppoe-ia:pppoe/bbf-pppoe-ia:out-packets-to-server
Description: Valid PPPoE discovery stage packets sent to the  interface. The packets are sent to a PPPoE Server  connected to it (directly or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  PPPoE Intermediate Agent functionality is disabled/  enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-pppoe-ia:pppoe/bbf-pppoe-ia:out-packets-to-client
Description: Valid PPPoE discovery stage packets sent to the  interface. The packets are sent to a PPPoE Client  connected to it (directly or indirectly through a network).    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  PPPoE Intermediate Agent functionality is disabled/  enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-pppoe-ia:pppoe/bbf-pppoe-ia:vendor-specific-tag-inserted-packets-to-server
Description: PPPoE discovery stage packets from a Client to a Server  that pass this interface and into which the PPPoE  Intermediate Agent functionality inserted the  vendor-specific tag.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  PPPoE Intermediate Agent functionality is disabled/  enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-pppoe-ia:pppoe/bbf-pppoe-ia:vendor-specific-tag-removed-packets-to-client
Description: PPPoE discovery stage packets from a Server to a Client  that pass this interface and from which the PPPoE  Intermediate Agent functionality removed the  vendor-specific tag.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  PPPoE Intermediate Agent functionality is disabled/  enabled.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/bbf-pppoe-ia:pppoe/bbf-pppoe-ia:outgoing-mtu-exceeded-packets-from-client
Description: PPPoE discovery stage packets from a Client that are  dropped because the PPPoE Message size after  adding the vendor-specific tag would exceed the MTU  of the outgoing interface.    Discontinuities in the counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and/or when the  PPPoE Intermediate Agent functionality is disabled/  enabled.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:last-change
Description: The time the '/hardware-state/component' list changed.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:name
Description: The name assigned to this component.    This name is not required to be the same as  entPhysicalName.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:class
Description: An indication of the general hardware type of the  component.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:description
Description: A textual description of component.  This node should  contain a string that identifies the manufacturer's name  for the component and should be set to a distinct value  for each version or model of the component.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:parent
Description: The name of the component that physically contains this  component.    If this leaf is not instantiated, it indicates that this  component is not contained in any other component.    In the event that a physical component is contained by  more than one physical component (e.g., double-wide  modules), this node contains the name of one of these  components.  An implementation MUST use the same name  every time this node is instantiated.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:parent-rel-pos
Description: An indication of the relative position of this child  component among all its sibling components.  Sibling  components are defined as components that share the same  instance values of each of the 'parent' and 'class'  nodes.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:contains-child
Description: The name of the contained component.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:hardware-rev
Description: The vendor-specific hardware revision string for the  component.  The preferred value is the hardware revision  identifier actually printed on the component itself (if  present).

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:serial-num
Description: The vendor-specific serial number string for the  component.  The preferred value is the serial number  string actually printed on the component itself (if  present).    If a serial number has been configured for this component  in /hardware/component/serial-num, this node contains the  configured value.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:mfg-name
Description: The name of the manufacturer of this physical component.  The preferred value is the manufacturer name string  actually printed on the component itself (if present).    Note that comparisons between instances of the model-name,  firmware-rev, software-rev, and the serial-num nodes are  only meaningful amongst component with the same value of  mfg-name.    If the manufacturer name string associated with the  physical component is unknown to the server, then this  node is not instantiated.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:model-name
Description: The vendor-specific model name identifier string  associated with this physical component.  The preferred  value is the customer-visible part number, which may be  printed on the component itself.    If the model name string associated with the physical  component is unknown to the server, then this node is not  instantiated.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:alias
Description: An 'alias' name for the component, as specified by a  network manager, and provides a non-volatile 'handle' for  the component.    If an alias has been configured for this component in  /hardware/component/alias, this node contains the  configured value.  If no such alias has been configured,  the server may set the value of this node to a locally  unique value.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:asset-id
Description: This node is a user-assigned asset tracking identifier for  the component.    If an asset tracking identifier has been configured for  this component in /hardware/component/asset-id, this node  contains the configured value.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:is-fru
Description: This node indicates whether or not this component is  considered a 'field replaceable unit' by the vendor.  If  this node contains the value 'true', then this component  identifies a field replaceable unit.  For all components  that are permanently contained within a field replaceable  unit, the value 'false' should be returned for this  node.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:mfg-date
Description: The date of manufacturing of the managed component.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:uri
Description: This node contains identification information about the  component.    If uris have been configured for this component in  /hardware/component/uri, this node contains the configured  values.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:state/hw:admin-state
Description: The administrative state for this component.    This node refers to a component's administrative  permission to service both other components within its  containment hierarchy as well other users of its  services defined by means outside the scope of this  module.  Some components exhibit only a subset of the remaining  administrative state values.  Some components cannot be  locked, and hence this node exhibits only the 'unlocked'  state.  Other components cannot be shutdown gracefully,  and hence this node does not exhibit the 'shutting-down'  state.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:state/hw:oper-state
Description: The operational state for this component.    Note that this node does not follow the administrative  state.  An administrative state of down does not predict  an operational state of disabled.    Note that some implementations may not be able to  accurately report oper-state while the admin-state node  has a value other than 'unlocked'.  In these cases, this  node MUST have a value of 'unknown'.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/hw:sensor-data/hw:value
Description: The most recent measurement obtained by the server  for this sensor.    A client that periodically fetches this node should also  fetch the nodes 'value-type', 'value-scale', and  'value-precision', since they may change when the value  is changed.

Name: /if:interfaces/if:interface/if:name
Description: The name of the interface.    A device MAY restrict the allowed values for this leaf,  possibly depending on the type of the interface.  For system-controlled interfaces, this leaf is the  device-specific name of the interface.  The 'config false'  list /interfaces-state/interface contains the currently  existing interfaces on the device.    If a client tries to create configuration for a  system-controlled interface that is not present in the  /interfaces-state/interface list, the server MAY reject  the request if the implementation does not support  pre-provisioning of interfaces or if the name refers to  an interface that can never exist in the system.  A  NETCONF server MUST reply with an rpc-error with the  error-tag 'invalid-value' in this case.    If the device supports pre-provisioning of interface  configuration, the 'pre-provisioning' feature is  advertised.    If the device allows arbitrarily named user-controlled  interfaces, the 'arbitrary-names' feature is advertised.    When a configured user-controlled interface is created by  the system, it is instantiated with the same name in the  /interface-state/interface list.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:name
Description: The name of the interface.    A server implementation MAY map this leaf to the ifName  MIB object.  Such an implementation needs to use some  mechanism to handle the differences in size and characters  allowed between this leaf and ifName.  The definition of  such a mechanism is outside the scope of this document.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:type
Description: The type of the interface.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:admin-status
Description: The desired state of the interface.    This leaf has the same read semantics as ifAdminStatus.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:oper-status
Description: The current operational state of the interface.    This leaf has the same semantics as ifOperStatus.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:last-change
Description: The time the interface entered its current operational  state.  If the current state was entered prior to the  last re-initialization of the local network management  subsystem, then this node is not present.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:if-index
Description: The ifIndex value for the ifEntry represented by this  interface.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:phys-address
Description: The interface's address at its protocol sub-layer.  For  example, for an 802.x interface, this object normally  contains a Media Access Control (MAC) address.  The  interface's media-specific modules must define the bit      and byte ordering and the format of the value of this  object.  For interfaces that do not have such an address  (e.g., a serial line), this node is not present.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:higher-layer-if
Description: A list of references to interfaces layered on top of this  interface.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:lower-layer-if
Description: A list of references to interfaces layered underneath this  interface.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:speed
Description: An estimate of the interface's current bandwidth in bits  per second.  For interfaces that do not vary in  bandwidth or for those where no accurate estimation can  be made, this node should contain the nominal bandwidth.  For interfaces that have no concept of bandwidth, this  node is not present.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:discontinuity-time
Description: The time on the most recent occasion at which any one or  more of this interface's counters suffered a  discontinuity.  If no such discontinuities have occurred  since the last re-initialization of the local management  subsystem, then this node contains the time the local  management subsystem re-initialized itself.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:in-octets
Description: The total number of octets received on the interface,  including framing characters.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:in-unicast-pkts
Description: The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a  higher (sub-)layer, that were not addressed to a  multicast or broadcast address at this sub-layer.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:in-broadcast-pkts
Description: The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a  higher (sub-)layer, that were addressed to a broadcast  address at this sub-layer.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:in-multicast-pkts
Description: The number of packets, delivered by this sub-layer to a  higher (sub-)layer, that were addressed to a multicast  address at this sub-layer.  For a MAC-layer protocol,  this includes both Group and Functional addresses.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:in-discards
Description: The number of inbound packets that were chosen to be  discarded even though no errors had been detected to  prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer  protocol.  One possible reason for discarding such a  packet could be to free up buffer space.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:in-errors
Description: For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of inbound  packets that contained errors preventing them from being  deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-  oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of  inbound transmission units that contained errors  preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer  protocol.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:in-unknown-protos
Description: For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of packets  received via the interface that were discarded because  of an unknown or unsupported protocol.  For  character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces that  support protocol multiplexing, the number of  transmission units received via the interface that were  discarded because of an unknown or unsupported protocol.  For any interface that does not support protocol  multiplexing, this counter is not present.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:out-octets
Description: The total number of octets transmitted out of the  interface, including framing characters.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:out-unicast-pkts
Description: The total number of packets that higher-level protocols  requested be transmitted, and that were not addressed  to a multicast or broadcast address at this sub-layer,  including those that were discarded or not sent.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:out-broadcast-pkts
Description: The total number of packets that higher-level protocols  requested be transmitted, and that were addressed to a  broadcast address at this sub-layer, including those  that were discarded or not sent.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:out-multicast-pkts
Description: The total number of packets that higher-level protocols  requested be transmitted, and that were addressed to a  multicast address at this sub-layer, including those  that were discarded or not sent.  For a MAC-layer  protocol, this includes both Group and Functional  addresses.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:out-discards
Description: The number of outbound packets that were chosen to be  discarded even though no errors had been detected to  prevent their being transmitted.  One possible reason  for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer  space.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/if:statistics/if:out-errors
Description: For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of outbound  packets that could not be transmitted because of errors.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the  number of outbound transmission units that could not be  transmitted because of errors.          Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/nokia-ipv4-sppr:ipv4-security-statistics/nokia-ipv4-sppr:interface/nokia-arpsec:out-arp-req-packet-forwards
Description: The number of broadcast ARP packets that are sent out of this  interface after the ARP 'secured-forwarding' selection of  this interface as the unique interface where the ARP packet  shall be sent.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'interfaces-state/interfaces/statistics/  discontinuity-time'.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/nokia-disk:nand-resource-data/nokia-disk:max-erase-cycles
Description: The maximum number times one sectory has been erased.  This number can increase slowly over time, a high  increasing number might lead to damage on the NAND storage.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/nokia-disk:nand-resource-data/nokia-disk:bad-sectors
Description: The number of bad, or unusable, sectors.  This number can increase slowly over time, a high  increasing number might lead to damage on the NAND storage.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/nokia-disk:volume-resource-data/nokia-disk:volume-resource-list/nokia-disk:size
Description: The total size of this volume or partition.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/nokia-disk:volume-resource-data/nokia-disk:volume-resource-list/nokia-disk:free
Description: The amount of free size of volume or partition.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver-link/bbf-hw-xcvr:diagnostics/nokia-hw-xcvr-dbm:tx-power-dbm
Description: Measured TX output power in dBm. Data is assumed to be  based on measurement of laser monitor photodiode current.  It is factory calibrated to absolute units using the most  representative fiber output type. Accuracy is vendor  specific but must be better than +/- 3dB over specified  temperature and voltage.

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver-link/bbf-hw-xcvr:diagnostics/nokia-hw-xcvr-dbm:rx-power-dbm
Description: Measured received (RX) optical power in dBm. Absolute  accuracy is dependent upon the exact optical wavelength.  For the vendor specified wavelength, accuracy shall be  better than +/- 3dB over specified temperature and  voltage. This accuracy shall be maintained for input  power levels up to the lesser of maximum transmitted or  maximum received optical power per the appropriate  standard. It shall be maintained down to the minimum  transmitted power minus cable plant loss (insertion  loss  or passive loss) per the appropriate standard. Absolute  accuracy beyond this minimum required received input  optical power range is vendor specific.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:in-drop-events
Description: The total number of events in which inbound packets were  dropped due to a lack of resources

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:in-total-pkts
Description: The total number of inbound packets received, including bad  packets, broadcast packets and multicast packets

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:in-crc-errored-pkts
Description: The total number of inbound packets received that had a length  (excluding framing bits, but including FCS octets) of between 64  octets and 1518 octets, inclusive, but had either a bad FCS with  an integral number of octets (FCS error) or a bad FCS with a  non-integral number of octets (alignment error)

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:in-undersize-pkts
Description: The total number of inbound packets received that were less than  64 octets long, but were otherwise well formed (excluding framing  bits, but including FCS)

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:in-oversize-pkts
Description: The total number of inbound packets received that were longer than  1518 octets (excluding framing bits, but including FCS) and were  otherwise well formed

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:in-64-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of inbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 64 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:in-65-to-127-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of inbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 65..127 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:in-128-to-255-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of inbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 128..255 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:in-256-to-511-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of inbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 256..511 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:in-512-to-1023-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of inbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 512..1023 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:in-1024-to-1518-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of inbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 1024..1518 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:out-drop-events
Description: The total number of events in which outbound packets were  dropped due to a lack of resources

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:out-total-pkts
Description: The total number of outbound packets received, including bad  packets, broadcast packets and multicast packets

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:out-crc-errored-pkts
Description: The total number of outbound packets received that had a length  (excluding framing bits, but including FCS octets) of between 64  octets and 1518 octets, inclusive, but had either a bad FCS with  an integral number of octets (FCS error) or a bad FCS with a  non-integral number of octets (alignment error)

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:out-undersize-pkts
Description: The total number of outbound packets received that were less than  64 octets long, but were otherwise well formed (excluding framing  bits, but including FCS)

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:out-oversize-pkts
Description: The total number of outbound packets received that were longer than  1518 octets (excluding framing bits, but including FCS) and were  otherwise well formed

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:out-64-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of outbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 64 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:out-65-to-127-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of outbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 65..127 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:out-128-to-255-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of outbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 128..255 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:out-256-to-511-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of outbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 256..511 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:out-512-to-1023-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of outbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 512..1023 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-interfaces-performance-management:out-1024-to-1518-octets-pkts
Description: The total number of outbound received packets (including bad packets)  that were 1024..1518 octets long, excluding framing bits but including FCS

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/nokia-sdan-hw-xcvr-status:inventory-status
Description: Indicates status of reading transceiver inventory  data from A0 bank of EEPROM

Name: /hw:hardware-state/hw:component/bbf-hw-xcvr:transceiver/bbf-hw-xcvr:diagnostics/nokia-sdan-hw-xcvr-status:status
Description: Indicates status of reading transceiver diagnostics  monitoring data from A2 bank of EEPROM.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-itfpktcnt:in-pkts
Description: The total number of packets that are received at this      sub-layer interface, including all unicast, multicast      and broadcast packets, irrespective of whether these      packets are actually being forwarded /discarded by      the higher layer.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/bbf-if-pm:performance/bbf-if-pm:intervals-15min/bbf-if-pm:history/nokia-itfpktcnt:out-pkts
Description: The total number of packets that are requested be      transmitted at this sub-layer interface, including all      unicast, multicast and broadcast packets, irrespective      of whether these packets are actually being sent or      are discarded at the lower level interface.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/nokia-ipv4-sppr:ipv4-security-statistics/nokia-ipv4-sppr:interface/nokia-ipv4-sppr:name
Description: The name of a VLAN sub-interface.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/nokia-ipv4-sppr:ipv4-security-statistics/nokia-ipv4-sppr:interface/nokia-ipv4-sppr:in-ip-packet-discards
Description: The number of IP packets received on the interface that  are discarded because of the source IP address of the  incoming packet is not assigned to the interface.    This counter includes the protocol packets processed by  the system, such as IGMP and DHCP, for which applies that  packets with the source IP adress 0.0.0.0 are valid. I.e.  such a control packet is discarded if the IP address is  not assigned to the interface and is different from  0.0.0.0.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'interfaces-state/interfaces/statistics/  discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/nokia-ipv4-sppr:ipv4-security-statistics/nokia-ipv4-sppr:interface/nokia-ipv4-sppr:in-arp-probe-discards
Description: The number of ARP packets received on the interface that  are discarded because the sender protocol address of the  packet contains all zeros. ARP Probe packets are not  supported.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'interfaces-state/interfaces/statistics/  discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/nokia-ipv4-sppr:ipv4-security-statistics/nokia-ipv4-sppr:interface/nokia-ipv4-sppr:in-arp-sender-ip-discards
Description: The number of ARP packets received on the interface that  are discarded because the protocol address of the sender  is not assigned to the interface.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'interfaces-state/interfaces/statistics/  discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/nokia-ipv4-sppr:ipv4-security-statistics/nokia-ipv4-sppr:interface/nokia-ipv4-sppr:in-arp-mac-mismatch-discards
Description: The number of ARP packets received on the interface that  are discarded because the packet's sender hardware address  and sender protocol address do not correspond to an IP address  and MAC address assigned to the interface.    These fields are matching if they correspond with a pair  that is logged during DHCP snooping. I.e. at DHCP session  establishment a DHCP client uses a particular MAC address  as chaddr. When the DHCP server assigns an IP address,  then this IP address is associated to this MAC address.  ARP packets are discarded if the sender hardware address  and sender protocol address are not associated to each  other, even when these fields are valid on their own.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'interfaces-state/interfaces/statistics/  discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/nokia-ipv4-sppr:ipv4-security-statistics/nokia-ipv4-sppr:interface/nokia-ipv4-sppr:in-arp-target-ip-discards
Description: The number of ARP packets received on the interface that  are discarded because the protocol address of the target  is assigned to the interface where the packet is  received, provided the protocol address of the sender and  protocol address of target are different.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'interfaces-state/interfaces/statistics/  discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/nokia-ipv4-sppr:ipv4-security-statistics/nokia-ipv4-sppr:interface/nokia-ipv4-sppr:in-garp-forwards
Description: The number of ARP packets received on the interface that  are forwarded and for which applies that:  - the protocol address of the sender equals the protocol    address of the target, and  - this address is assigned to the interface where the    packet is received.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'interfaces-state/interfaces/statistics/  discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/nokia-ipv4-sppr:ipv4-security-statistics/nokia-ipv4-sppr:interface/nokia-ipv4-sppr:in-arp-source-mac-senderhwaddr-mismatch-discards
Description: The number of ARP packets received on the interface that  are discarded because the sender hardware address which  is available in the packet payload does not match with  the source MAC address which is available in the packet  Ethernet Header.    Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur  at re-initialization of the management system, and at  other times as indicated by the value of  'interfaces-state/interfaces/statistics/  discontinuity-time'.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-queue-mon:tm-root/nokia-queue-mon:queue/nokia-queue-mon:performance/nokia-queue-mon:intervals-15min/nokia-queue-mon:number-of-intervals
Description: The total number of 15-minute PM intervals for which data  was collected.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-queue-mon:tm-root/nokia-queue-mon:queue/nokia-queue-mon:performance/nokia-queue-mon:intervals-15min/nokia-queue-mon:non-valid-intervals
Description: The number of 15-minute PM intervals for which the data  is considered to be invalid or incomplete.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-queue-mon:tm-root/nokia-queue-mon:queue/nokia-queue-mon:performance/nokia-queue-mon:intervals-15min/nokia-queue-mon:history/nokia-queue-mon:interval-number
Description: The number of the interval relative to the current  interval.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-queue-mon:tm-root/nokia-queue-mon:queue/nokia-queue-mon:performance/nokia-queue-mon:intervals-15min/nokia-queue-mon:history/nokia-queue-mon:measured-time
Description: Reports the amount of time, measured in seconds,  that statistics for this interval have been counted.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-queue-mon:tm-root/nokia-queue-mon:queue/nokia-queue-mon:performance/nokia-queue-mon:intervals-15min/nokia-queue-mon:history/nokia-queue-mon:invalid-data-flag
Description: Reports the validity of the corresponding PM data.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-queue-mon:tm-root/nokia-queue-mon:queue/nokia-queue-mon:performance/nokia-queue-mon:intervals-15min/nokia-queue-mon:history/nokia-queue-mon:time-stamp
Description: Reports the start date/time for this interval.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-queue-mon:tm-root/nokia-queue-mon:queue/nokia-queue-mon:performance/nokia-queue-mon:intervals-15min/nokia-queue-mon:history/nokia-queue-mon:out-pkts
Description: Number of packets transmitted from the queue.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-queue-mon:tm-root/nokia-queue-mon:queue/nokia-queue-mon:performance/nokia-queue-mon:intervals-15min/nokia-queue-mon:history/nokia-queue-mon:out-octets
Description: Number of octets transmitted from the queue.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-queue-mon:tm-root/nokia-queue-mon:queue/nokia-queue-mon:performance/nokia-queue-mon:intervals-15min/nokia-queue-mon:history/nokia-queue-mon:drop-pkts
Description: Total number of packets that were addressed to the queue, but  that were dropped instead of being queued, e.g. because of  buffer overflow.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-queue-mon:tm-root/nokia-queue-mon:queue/nokia-queue-mon:performance/nokia-queue-mon:intervals-15min/nokia-queue-mon:history/nokia-queue-mon:drop-octets
Description: Total number of octets that were addressed to the queue, but  that were dropped instead of being queued, e.g. because of  buffer overflow.

Name: /lic-app:licensing-state/lic-app:features/lic-app:feature-code
Description: The code for this feature, as defined in the license key installed in CSWL.

Name: /lic-app:licensing-state/lic-app:features/lic-app:feature-name
Description: The name of this feature, as defined in the license key installed in CSWL.

Name: /lic-app:licensing-state/lic-app:features/lic-app:grace-period-end
Description: Displays the date-and-time the feature code's grace period expires,  if the the grace period is running for this feature. If no grace period is running,  then this field will be left empty.

Name: /lic-app:licensing-state/lic-app:features/lic-app:type
Description: The type of the floating feature (either on/off or capacity).

Name: /lic-app:licensing-state/lic-app:features/lic-app:total-requested
Description: The requested number of items for this feature (type: capacity). Empty if (type: on/off).

Name: /lic-app:licensing-state/lic-app:features/lic-app:total-granted
Description: Number of granted license keys.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:cache/ipfix:name
Description: Key of this list.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-itf-tm:speed-monitoring/nokia-itf-tm:data/nokia-itf-tm:monitoring-period-length
Description: The period over which the data is collected.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-itf-tm:speed-monitoring/nokia-itf-tm:data/nokia-itf-tm:number-of-intervals
Description: The total number of speed-monitoring-history intervals  for which data was collected. It contains the number of  non-valid intervals.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-itf-tm:speed-monitoring/nokia-itf-tm:data/nokia-itf-tm:history/nokia-itf-tm:interval-number
Description: The position of a completed interval relative to the  current interval. Value '1' corresponds to the last  interval completed

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/nokia-queue-mon:tm-root/nokia-queue-mon:queue/nokia-queue-mon:local-queue-id
Description: The identification of a queue within the context of  this list.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/ip:ipv4/ip:address/ip:ip
Description: The IPv4 address on the interface.

Name: /if:interfaces-state/if:interface/ip:ipv6/ip:address/ip:ip
Description: The IPv6 address on the interface.

Name: /sys:system/sys:contact
Description: The administrator contact information for the system.    A server implementation MAY map this leaf to the sysContact  MIB object.  Such an implementation needs to use some  mechanism to handle the differences in size and characters  allowed between this leaf and sysContact.  The definition of  such a mechanism is outside the scope of this document.

Name: /sys:system/sys:hostname
Description: The name of the host.  This name can be a single domain  label or the fully qualified domain name of the host.

Name: /sys:system/sys:location
Description: The system location.    A server implementation MAY map this leaf to the sysLocation  MIB object.  Such an implementation needs to use some  mechanism to handle the differences in size and characters  allowed between this leaf and sysLocation.  The definition  of such a mechanism is outside the scope of this document.

Name: /sys:system-state/sys:clock/sys:current-datetime
Description: The current system date and time.

Name: /sys:system-state/sys:clock/sys:boot-datetime
Description: The system date and time when the system last restarted.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:cache/ipfix:dataRecords
Description: The number of Data Records generated by this  Cache.  Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and at other  times as indicated by the value of  cacheDiscontinuityTime.  Note that this parameter corresponds to  ipfixMeteringProcessDataRecords in the IPFIX MIB  module.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:cache/ipfix:cacheDiscontinuityTime
Description: Timestamp of the most recent occasion at which  the counter dataRecords suffered a discontinuity.  Note that this parameter functionally corresponds to  ipfixMeteringProcessDiscontinuityTime in the IPFIX MIB  module.  In contrast to  ipfixMeteringProcessDiscontinuityTime, the time is  absolute and not relative to sysUpTime.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:name
Description: Key of this list.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:destination/ipfix:name
Description: Key of this list.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:destination/ipfix:tcpExporter/ipfix:transportSession/ipfix:ipfixVersion
Description: Used for Exporting Processes, this parameter  contains the version number of the IPFIX protocol that the  Exporter uses to export its data in this Transport Session.  Hence, it is identical to the value of the configuration  parameter ipfixVersion of the outer SctpExporter,  UdpExporter, or TcpExporter node.  Used for Collecting Processes, this parameter contains the  version number of the IPFIX protocol it receives for  this Transport Session.  If IPFIX Messages of different  IPFIX protocol versions are received, this parameter  contains the maximum version number.  Note that this parameter corresponds to  ipfixTransportSessionIpfixVersion in the IPFIX MIB  module.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:destination/ipfix:tcpExporter/ipfix:transportSession/ipfix:destinationAddress
Description: The destination address of the Collector of  the IPFIX Transport Session.  If the transport protocol is SCTP, this is one of the  potentially many IP addresses of the Collector.  Preferably, the destination IP address of the path that is  usually selected by the Exporter to send IPFIX Messages to  the Collector SHOULD be used.  Note that this parameter functionally corresponds to  ipfixTransportSessionDestinationAddressType and  ipfixTransportSessionDestinationAddress in the IPFIX MIB  module.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:destination/ipfix:tcpExporter/ipfix:transportSession/ipfix:destinationPort
Description: The transport-protocol port number of the  Collector of the IPFIX Transport Session.  Note that this parameter corresponds to  ipfixTransportSessionDestinationPort in the IPFIX MIB  module.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:destination/ipfix:tcpExporter/ipfix:transportSession/ipfix:status
Description: Status of the Transport Session.  Note that this parameter corresponds to  ipfixTransportSessionStatus in the IPFIX MIB module.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:destination/ipfix:tcpExporter/ipfix:transportSession/ipfix:bytes
Description: The number of bytes transmitted by the  Exporting Process or received by the Collecting Process.  Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and at other  times as indicated by the value of  transportSessionDiscontinuityTime.  Note that this parameter corresponds to  ipfixTransportSessionBytes in the IPFIX MIB module.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:destination/ipfix:tcpExporter/ipfix:transportSession/ipfix:messages
Description: The number of messages transmitted by the  Exporting Process or received by the Collecting Process.  Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and at other  times as indicated by the value of  transportSessionDiscontinuityTime.  Note that this parameter corresponds to  ipfixTransportSessionMessages in the IPFIX MIB module.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:destination/ipfix:tcpExporter/ipfix:transportSession/ipfix:discardedMessages
Description: Used for Exporting Processes, this parameter  indicates the number of messages that could not be sent due  to internal buffer overflows, network congestion, routing  issues, etc.  Used for Collecting Process, this parameter  indicates the number of received IPFIX Message that are  malformed, cannot be decoded, are received in the wrong  order or are missing according to the sequence number.  Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and at other  times as indicated by the value of  transportSessionDiscontinuityTime.  Note that this parameter corresponds to  ipfixTransportSessionDiscardedMessages in the IPFIX MIB  module.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:destination/ipfix:tcpExporter/ipfix:transportSession/ipfix:records
Description: The number of Data Records transmitted by the  Exporting Process or received by the Collecting Process.  Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and at other  times as indicated by the value of  transportSessionDiscontinuityTime.  Note that this parameter corresponds to  ipfixTransportSessionRecords in the IPFIX MIB module.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:destination/ipfix:tcpExporter/ipfix:transportSession/ipfix:templates
Description: The number of Templates transmitted by the  Exporting Process or received by the Collecting Process.  Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at  re-initialization of the management system, and at other  times as indicated by the value of  transportSessionDiscontinuityTime.  Note that this parameter corresponds to  ipfixTransportSessionTemplates in the IPFIX MIB module.

Name: /ipfix:ipfix/ipfix:exportingProcess/ipfix:destination/ipfix:tcpExporter/ipfix:transportSession/ipfix:optionsTemplates
Description: The number of Option Templates transmitted by the  Exporting Process or received by the Collecting Process.
